items.title: How to disable ```auto expanding(and select langange) in the forum post?
items.author: zw963
items.content:
I don’t remember when crystal forum update to support this, I really hate it.

What I expected is , I type the beginning/ending of code use ``` myself, can I disable this to rollback to old forum behavior?

items.title: OptionParser parse lazy
items.author: zw963
items.content:
I have some concern to support sub command, so, I have to change opt parser dynamically.

opt = OptionParser.new

opt.on(“-r”, “–root PATH”, “The path to the root of your application”) do |root|  
  options[:root] = root
end

opt.parse

# here, the ARGV is the stripped version of ARGV, which remove the -r PATH part.

command = ARGV[0]? || “help”

# Now, I need add parse sub command logic depend on the above command value

if command == “start”
  opts.on(“-f”,“–foreground”,“Run the supervisor in the foreground”) do
    # do something
  end
end

# What I expected is opt get parse again to output the expected opts -r(globally) + -f(for start command)

opt.parse

The issue for current impletement is:

It will eagly print the opt options when I first run opt.parse.

Something what I expected is:

opt = OptionParser.new

opt.on(“-r”, “–root PATH”, “The path to the root of your application”) do |root| 
  options[:root] = root
end

opt.update # only parse and update ARGV

# check ARGV and optional update opts
if ARGV[0] == “start”
  opts.on(“-f”,“–foreground”,“Run the supervisor in the foreground”) do
    # …
  end
end

opt.update

opt.parse # print the final options

I don’t see a solution for current implement, any idea?

What I really want is to support parse like this: (-r is global option, -f is for start subcommand only)

bin/procodile -r spec/apps/basic/ start -f

If support this, I can add a alias proc1="procodile -r spec/apps/basic", then use like this: proc1 start -f, for now, only support use like: bin/procodile start -r spec/apps/basic/ -f`
